title: Strategy & Mobilize
summary: Aligns business outcomes, modernization priorities, governance posture, and organizational readiness before large-scale agentic delivery begins.
whyItMatters: Enterprise modernization fails when strategy, sponsorship, governance, and delivery expectations are vague. This phase establishes the value case, decision model, and mobilization plan so agentic engineering is anchored in business priorities rather than tool enthusiasm.
keyDecisions: What business outcomes and modernization goals matter most?
Which domains, products, or value streams are the right entry point?
What governance posture and risk tiering are required?
What operating model will govern the work?
What capabilities must be in place before execution scales?
majorActivities: Define the business case and target outcomes
Assess modernization scope and domain candidates
Establish executive sponsorship and decision rights
Set governance posture, policies, and autonomy boundaries
Create rollout waves, success criteria, and mobilization roadmap

title: Design
summary: Uses domain-driven design, event storming, modernization patterns, and agent design to understand the system, define boundaries, and shape the target-state solution.
whyItMatters: Design is where large-system modernization becomes intentional instead of reactive. This phase defines the business capabilities, boundaries, flows, interfaces, modernization patterns, and agent roles that guide everything downstream.
keyDecisions: What are the bounded contexts, capabilities, and seams for modernization?
What inputs, outputs, events, screens, and workflows define the domain?
What modernization patterns apply to each part of the system?
What roles will humans and agents play in the target process?
What artifacts are required for implementation and governance?
majorActivities: Run DDD and event storming workshops
Document user journeys, screens, inputs, outputs, and events
Define bounded contexts and integration patterns
Select target architectures and modernization patterns
Design agent roles, handoffs, and review requirements

title: Build
summary: Implements the solution through structured human-agent collaboration across code, tests, security, documentation, and architecture conformance.
whyItMatters: Build is where the operating model turns into repeatable engineering work. It must balance acceleration with reviewability, traceability, quality, and control.
keyDecisions: What work can be delegated to agents and what must remain human-led?
What artifacts will structure work decomposition and review?
What standards govern code, tests, security, and documentation?
How will context be supplied consistently to humans and agents?
What evidence is required before work is considered ready?
majorActivities: Decompose work into structured implementation packets
Generate and refine code, tests, and documentation
Run architecture conformance and security checks
Perform human review on risk-tiered changes
Capture evidence packs for release readiness

title: Release & Adopt
summary: Transitions work into production with governed pipelines, evidence-based approvals, progressive rollout, and adoption support for teams and users.
whyItMatters: Release is where speed and trust are tested. This phase ensures that delivery is observable, controlled, and ready for real users without collapsing under governance or handoff friction.
keyDecisions: What approvals are required by release risk tier?
What evidence is needed before promotion?
What rollout and rollback strategy should be used?
How will end users and client teams be onboarded?
What signals determine whether a release is healthy?
majorActivities: Provision environments and validate readiness
Run gated CI/CD and deployment workflows
Collect and review evidence for approval
Execute progressive rollout and rollback plans
Train users, operators, and client teams on the release

title: Operate & Evolve
summary: Runs the system with observability, SRE automation, incident learning, backlog refinement, and continuous modernization rather than treating go-live as the finish line.
whyItMatters: The model only becomes credible when it improves live-system performance and continuously evolves the product, platform, and governance. Operations are where long-term value and trust are proven.
keyDecisions: What runtime telemetry and health signals matter most?
Which operational tasks should be agent-assisted?
How will incidents, drift, and defects feed the backlog?
When should autonomy be reduced, expanded, or requalified?
How will modernization continue after the first release?
majorActivities: Monitor reliability, cost, compliance, and agent behavior
Use SRE and triage agents for anomaly detection and incident support
Feed incidents and telemetry into backlog refinement
Refactor, remediate, and modernize based on evidence
Requalify agents, controls, and operating procedures over time
